figures.caption: Figure 1: A schematic view of the proposed method.
Table 1: Experimental configurations for the proposed framework used for each physical dynamics.
Figure 2: A schematic view of the experimental flow.
Table 2: Hyperparameters used for the symbolic-regression experiments.
Table 3: Sparsity-dependent equation discovery performance across physical dynamics, symbolic regression backends, and data-enrichment variants.

Table 4: Synthetic-only equation recovery performance. Results are computed on fully synthetic sequences generated either by the LDM or by the physics-corrected model (PhysLDM). Lower MSE is better, and higher R2 is better. Bold indicates the best value within each physical dynamics and SR model.

Table 5: Generated-data fidelity and physical validity metrics. GT/reference values are included where applicable. Lower is better for error and residual metrics, while higher is better for R2 and correlation metrics. Bold indicates the better generated result between LDM and PhysLDM.
